description.task: Implement a full generic-style ArrayStack class backed by a resizable int array. When the array fills up, double its capacity. Support push, pop, peek, and size operations.
instructions: push: call resize() if full (already done above TODO), then data[++top]=val
pop: if top==-1, throw new RuntimeException('Stack is empty'); return data[top--]
resize() is already implemented — it doubles capacity using System.arraycopy
Initial capacity is 4; pushing 6 elements triggers one resize to capacity 8
After push(10..60): size=6, peek=60, pop=60, pop=50, size=4
description.hint: push: data[++top]=val; pop: if(top==-1) throw new RuntimeException('Stack is empty'); return data[top--];
description.expectedOutput:
Size: 6
Peek: 60
Pop: 60
Pop: 50
Size after pops: 4

description.task: Implement a LinkedStack class backed by a singly linked list of nodes. Each push adds a node at the head and each pop removes from the head. Demonstrate that size is unlimited (no capacity constraint).
instructions: push: top = new Node(val, top); size++;
pop: if(top==null) throw RuntimeException; int v=top.val; top=top.next; size--; return v;
peek(), isEmpty(), size(), and print() are already implemented
After push(1,2,3): print shows TOP -> 3 -> 2 -> 1; peek=3
After pop() removes 3: print shows TOP -> 2 -> 1; size=2
description.hint: push: top=new Node(val,top); size++. pop: if(top==null) throw new RuntimeException('Empty'); int v=top.val; top=top.next; size--; return v;

description.task: Implement a TwoStacksInArray class that fits two stacks into a single fixed-size array. Stack 1 grows from the left (index 0), Stack 2 grows from the right (index n-1). Push/pop operations must specify which stack.
instructions: push stack 2: data[--top2]=val (top2 starts at capacity, decrements inward)
pop stack 2: if top2==data.length throw RuntimeException; return data[top2++]
The guard in push (top1+1==top2) prevents overlap — do not modify it
After push(1,10), push(1,20): top1=1, data[0]=10, data[1]=20
After push(2,100), push(2,200): top2=8, data[9]=100, data[8]=200
description.hint: push2: data[--top2]=val. pop2: if(top2==data.length) throw RuntimeException; return data[top2++];
description.expectedOutput:
S1 peek: 20
S2 peek: 200
S1 pop: 20
S2 pop: 200
